questions.q: In which field did Yellapragada Subbarow primarily work?
questions.explanation: Subbarow was a biochemist whose work shaped the chemistry of cellular energy and anticancer drugs.

questions.q: Yellapragada Subbarow was born in which country?
questions.explanation: Subbarow was born in India before moving to the United States to pursue his scientific career.

questions.q: Subbarow's research helped establish the role of which molecule as the cell's energy currency?
questions.explanation: His work on phosphorus compounds in muscle helped reveal ATP and phosphocreatine as central to cellular energy.

questions.q: Which widely used cancer drug is associated with Subbarow's research?
questions.explanation: Subbarow's folic acid antagonist research led to methotrexate, a foundational chemotherapy drug.

questions.q: Subbarow is credited with the first synthesis of which essential vitamin?
questions.explanation: Subbarow led the team that first synthesized folic acid, a B-vitamin vital for cell growth.

questions.q: To which country did Subbarow emigrate to carry out most of his scientific career?
questions.explanation: Subbarow moved to the United States in the 1920s, where he conducted his major research.

questions.q: Subbarow's famous early method measured the amount of which element in body tissues?
questions.explanation: The Fiske–Subbarow method was a sensitive technique for measuring phosphorus in biological samples.

questions.q: Methotrexate, linked to Subbarow's work, is mainly used to treat what kind of disease?
questions.explanation: Methotrexate is an anticancer (and later anti-inflammatory) drug derived from folic acid antagonist research.

questions.q: What is the common name for adenosine triphosphate, central to Subbarow's findings?
questions.explanation: Adenosine triphosphate is abbreviated ATP, the universal energy carrier in living cells.

questions.q: Subbarow is best remembered as a pioneer who developed important new what?
questions.explanation: He is celebrated for developing key drugs and biochemical analytical methods.

questions.q: The analytical method co-developed by Subbarow for measuring phosphorus is named after him and which collaborator?
questions.explanation: The Fiske–Subbarow method, devised with Cyrus Fiske, measured inorganic phosphate colorimetrically.

questions.q: At which institution did Subbarow do his foundational work on ATP and phosphocreatine?
questions.explanation: Subbarow worked in the biochemistry department at Harvard Medical School in the 1920s and early 1930s.

questions.q: Subbarow identified which phosphorus-containing compound that stores energy in muscle?
questions.explanation: He helped discover phosphocreatine, an energy-storing phosphate compound in muscle tissue.

questions.q: Which pharmaceutical company did Subbarow join, where he later directed major drug research?
questions.explanation: Subbarow joined Lederle Laboratories (a division of American Cyanamid), leading its research programs.

questions.q: Subbarow's team developed the first effective folic acid antagonist used against childhood leukemia. What class of drug is this?
questions.explanation: Antifolates like aminopterin and methotrexate block folate metabolism, halting cancer cell growth.

questions.q: Aminopterin, developed under Subbarow's direction, was famously used by Sidney Farber to achieve what milestone?
options: First temporary remissions in childhood leukemia
questions.explanation: Farber used aminopterin to induce the first remissions in children with acute leukemia in 1947–48.

questions.q: Subbarow's lab also produced an early antifilarial drug effective against the parasitic disease elephantiasis. What is the drug?
questions.explanation: Diethylcarbamazine, marketed as Hetrazan, came from Subbarow's program and is used against filariasis.

questions.q: Subbarow's group also worked on isolating and developing which broad-spectrum antibiotic family?
questions.explanation: Lederle under Subbarow developed Aureomycin (chlortetracycline), the first tetracycline antibiotic.

questions.q: In which decade was folic acid first synthesized by Subbarow's team?
questions.explanation: Subbarow's team achieved the first synthesis of folic acid in the mid-1940s.

questions.q: Despite his landmark contributions, Subbarow notably did NOT receive which recognition often cited as an oversight?
questions.explanation: Subbarow never received a Nobel Prize, and his contributions were long underrecognized in his lifetime.

questions.q: Subbarow earned an advanced graduate degree in biochemistry from which prestigious institution?
questions.explanation: Subbarow obtained his PhD in biochemistry from Harvard, where he also conducted early research.

questions.q: The Fiske–Subbarow method detects inorganic phosphate by producing a colored complex measured how?
options: By counting crystals under a microscope
questions.explanation: The method reduces a phosphomolybdate complex to a blue color whose absorbance indicates phosphate concentration.

questions.q: Subbarow's discovery of phosphocreatine and ATP overturned which earlier view of muscle energy?
options: That lactic acid alone powered contraction
That muscles burned pure glucose only
That nerves not muscles stored energy
questions.explanation: His work showed phosphate compounds, not just lactic acid, were central to muscle energy metabolism.

questions.q: Aminopterin and methotrexate work by inhibiting which enzyme in folate metabolism?
questions.explanation: Antifolates block dihydrofolate reductase, depriving rapidly dividing cells of reduced folate for DNA synthesis.

questions.q: Subbarow initially studied to enter which profession in India before turning to research?
questions.explanation: Subbarow trained in medicine in India, earning a medical qualification before pursuing biochemistry abroad.

questions.q: In what year did Yellapragada Subbarow die?
questions.explanation: Subbarow died in 1948, at the height of his contributions to drug development.

questions.q: The antibiotic Aureomycin developed at Subbarow's lab was isolated from which kind of organism?
questions.explanation: Aureomycin (chlortetracycline) was isolated from the soil bacterium Streptomyces aureofaciens.

questions.q: Why is methotrexate considered more clinically enduring than its predecessor aminopterin?
options: It is cheaper to make only
It had a more favorable therapeutic profile and broader uses
questions.explanation: Methotrexate proved safer and more versatile, becoming a mainstay in cancer and autoimmune therapy.

questions.q: Subbarow's early career in the U.S. was marked by hardship partly because he initially lacked what?
options: A recognized full medical license and steady funding
questions.explanation: He arrived with limited means and credentials not fully recognized, taking menial jobs before establishing his research.

questions.q: Which statement best captures Subbarow's overall scientific legacy?
options: His work underpinned ATP energy chemistry and yielded folic acid and methotrexate
questions.explanation: Subbarow's discoveries spanned cellular energy chemistry and pioneering drugs like folic acid and methotrexate.
